profile.required_fields: First name and last name are required.

profile.height_range: Birth height must be between 25 and 65 cm.
profile.gestational_range: Gestational age must be between 22 and 42 weeks.

feeding.tip_0_6mo: Exclusive breastfeeding for 6 months is recommended by DOH Philippines and WHO. 🤱
feeding.tip_4_6mo: Ready to start solids? Begin with single-ingredient purees like kamote or kalabasa! 🥕
feeding.tip_6_12mo: Lugaw with malunggay is a great iron-rich meal for your baby at this stage! 🌿
feeding.tip_12mo: Your toddler can now enjoy soft family foods in bite-sized pieces. 🍽️

sleep.title: Sleep Tracker
sleep.delete_body: This entry will be permanently removed. This action cannot be undone.
sleep.empty_sub: Track baby's first snooze by tapping the moon button above.
sleep.debt_short: 💤 Short by {{duration}} today — try an earlier bedtime

chart.guide_line1: Each bar = one day's total sleep. Number above = total hours (e.g. "8h").
chart.guide_line2: Dark = night sleep · purple top = daytime naps.
chart.guide_line3: Yellow dashed line = WHO minimum ({{hours}}h/day for baby's age).
chart.guide_line4: Green = great · Yellow = getting there · Pink = needs more rest.

sleep.ai_disclaimer: This analysis is based on WHO and DOH Philippines guidelines for general reference only. Please consult your Pediatrician for medical concerns.

tips.0_3: Newborns need 14–17 hours of sleep per day (WHO). Short wake windows of 45–60 minutes are normal.
tips.4_11: Babies this age need 12–15 hours daily including 2–3 naps. Watch for sleep cues like eye rubbing.
tips.12_23: Toddlers need 11–14 hours. One midday nap is ideal. Consistent bedtime routines help greatly.
tips.24_plus: Your child needs 11–14 hours of sleep daily. A consistent bedtime of 7–8 PM supports school readiness.

growth.percentile_explain_body: A percentile shows how your baby compares to others the same age. 50th = average. 15th–85th is the healthy green zone.
growth.who_source: Source: WHO Multicentre Growth Reference Study
growth.who_tip: WHO standards track weight, height, and head circumference to monitor healthy growth from 0–24 months.
growth.ai_summary_prompt: Tap View Full Analysis to see Ate AI growth insights.
growth.ai_add_prompt: Add your baby's measurements to get an AI growth analysis from Ate AI.

insights.title: Reports & Insights 📊

vaccine_kb.no_results: No vaccines found. Try a different search.
vaccine_kb.epi_banner: 10+ vaccines are FREE at every BHS/RHU in the Philippines. Bring your MCH Booklet!
vaccine_kb.ai_section_why: 🔬 Why It Matters for Filipino Babies
vaccine_kb.ai_disclaimer: Based on WHO, DOH Philippines EPI, and PPS guidelines. Always consult your Pedia for personalized advice.
vaccine_kb.resources_sub: Evidence-based guides from DOH, WHO & PPS
vaccine_kb.res_1_title: Free EPI Vaccines: Know Your Rights 🟢
vaccine_kb.res_1_body:
Under the DOH Expanded Program on Immunization (EPI), all Filipino children are entitled to 10+ life-saving vaccines completely FREE at any Barangay Health Station (BHS) or Rural Health Unit (RHU) nationwide.

Free EPI vaccines include: BCG, Hepatitis B, Pentavalent (DPT-HepB-Hib), OPV, IPV, PCV, Rotavirus, and MMR.

💡 Tip: Always bring your MCH (Mother & Child Health) Booklet. This is the official vaccination record required for school enrollment.

📍 Find your nearest BHS: Visit your local barangay hall or call the DOH Hotline 1-800-10-DOH-11-800 (toll-free).
vaccine_kb.res_2_body:
When a vaccine is given, it introduces a harmless version of a disease germ into your baby body. This trains the immune system to recognize and fight the real disease if encountered later.

Think of it as a practice drill 💪 - your baby immune cells learn to identify the enemy without getting sick.

After vaccination, mild symptoms like low fever or fussiness are normal and show the immune system is working! Usually resolves in 1-2 days.

Some vaccines need multiple doses to build strong, long-lasting immunity. Completing the full series is essential.
vaccine_kb.res_3_body:
Mild reactions after vaccines are normal. Philippine Pediatric Society (PPS) recommendations:

✅ NORMAL: Low-grade fever (below 38.5°C), mild fussiness, soreness at injection site, mild rash 7-14 days after MMR.

💊 For fever: Give paracetamol at the correct dose for baby weight. Ask your Pedia for the exact dose.

🤱 Breastfeeding: Continue! Research shows it reduces post-vaccine pain significantly.

⚠️ Call Pedia if: Fever above 38.5°C, persistent crying 3+ hours, unusual swelling, difficulty breathing, or seizures.
vaccine_kb.res_4_body:
Myth: Vaccines cause autism. FACT: Completely disproven. The original 1998 study was fraudulent and retracted. Hundreds of large studies confirm NO link. WHO, AAP, and PPS all confirm vaccines are safe.

Myth: Natural immunity is better. FACT: Natural immunity risks serious complications. Polio can cause permanent paralysis; measles can cause brain damage. Vaccines give immunity safely.

Myth: Too many vaccines overwhelm the immune system. FACT: Your baby immune system handles thousands of antigens daily. Vaccines are no challenge.

Myth: My baby doesn't need vaccines if neighbors vaccinate. FACT: Herd immunity only works at 95%+ vaccination. Your child still needs their own protection.
vaccine_kb.res_5_body:
Missed a vaccine? Do not worry - it is never too late to catch up! The DOH has a flexible catch-up immunization schedule.

📋 What to do: Visit nearest BHS/RHU or pediatrician. Bring MCH Booklet. A health worker will create a catch-up plan.

✅ No need to restart: If your child received 1st and 2nd doses of Pentavalent but missed 3rd, you only need the 3rd dose.

🟢 Free catch-up: Missed EPI vaccines are still free at BHS/RHU.

🇵🇭 Garantisadong Pambata: Each January and July, DOH runs free immunization drives at all BHS!
